segments.text: By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down, yes, we wept when we remembered Zion.
On the willows in the midst of it we hung up our lyres.
For there those who carried us away captive required of us a song,
And those who wasted us required mirth, saying, 'Sing us one of the songs of Zion!'
segments.speaker: Babylonian Captors

footnotes: a song: Heb. the words of a song
wasted…: Heb. laid us on heaps

segments.text: How shall we sing the Lord's song in a foreign land?
segments.speaker: Israelite Exiles

footnotes: strange…: Heb. land of a stranger?

segments.text: If I forget you, O Jerusalem, let my right hand forget its skill!
segments.speaker: Israelite Exiles

segments.text: If I do not remember you, let my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth—if I do not exalt Jerusalem above my chief joy.
segments.speaker: Israelite Exiles

footnotes: my chief…: Heb. the head of my joy

segments.text: Remember, O Lord, against the Edomites the day of Jerusalem, who said, 'Raze it, raze it, to its very foundation!'
segments.speaker: Israelite Exiles

segments.text: O daughter of Babylon, who are to be destroyed, happy shall he be who repays you as you have served us!
segments.speaker: Israelite Exiles

footnotes: rewardeth…: Heb. recompenseth unto thee thy deed which thou didst to us

segments.text: Happy shall he be, who takes and dashes your little ones against the stones.
segments.speaker: Israelite Exiles
